# Research Dossier: Mi-24P Hind Weapons & Systems

**Source Notes:**
- Primary data based on DCS World Mi-24P Hind module (Eagle Dynamics).
- Soviet/Russian Mi-24P weapon manuals used for comparison.
- **Discrepancy Note:** The 'P' variant differs from the 'V' in that it uses a fixed twin-barrel GSh-30K 30mm cannon on the starboard side rather than the Yak-B 12.7mm rotary cannon. Weapons Officer (front seat) operates most weapons systems. Pilot operates cannon and some rockets.

## Weapon/System Categories

### Gun/Cannon
| Weapon | Type | Effective Range | Best Suited For | Simulation Constraints |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **GSh-30K (twin-barrel 30mm)** | Fixed cannon (starboard) | < 1.5 km | Light Armor, APCs, Trucks. | Fixed mount — must aim entire aircraft. Pilot fires. High recoil. 250 rounds. |

### Anti-Tank Guided Missiles
| Weapon | Type | Effective Range | Best Suited For | Simulation Constraints |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **9M114 Shturm (AT-6 Spiral)** | Radio-Command Guided | 400m - 5 km | **Primary Anti-Tank.** MBTs, IFVs, Bunkers. | Requires Radiopeleng (weapons operator keeps crosshair on target). Must fly steady. Slow missile (~345 m/s). |
| **9M120 Ataka (AT-9 Spiral-2)** | Radio-Command Guided | 400m - 6 km | Improved Shturm. Heavier warhead. | Same guidance as Shturm but faster (~550 m/s) and longer range. |

### Rockets (Unguided)
| Weapon | Type | Effective Range | Best Suited For | Simulation Constraints |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **S-8 (80mm)** | Unguided HE/HEAT | 1-2 km | Soft targets, Light armor. | 20-round B-8V20A pods. CCIP or manual dive aiming. |
| **S-13 (122mm)** | Unguided HE | 1-2.5 km | Bunkers, Light Vehicles. | 5-round O-25 pods. Heavier punch per round. |
| **S-24 (240mm)** | Heavy unguided rocket | 2-3 km | Hardened targets, vehicles. | Single rocket per rail. Very powerful but inaccurate without laser ranging. |

### Air-to-Air Missiles
| Weapon | Type | Effective Range | Best Suited For | Simulation Constraints |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **9M39 Igla (SA-18 'Grouse')** | IR Homing (MANPADS adapted) | 0.5 - 5 km | Self-defense vs helicopters and low-flying aircraft. | All-aspect IR. Carried on external rail adapters. Limited quantity. |

### Bombs
| Weapon | Type | Effective Range | Best Suited For | Simulation Constraints |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **FAB-100 / FAB-250** | GP Bomb | Overflight | Soft area targets, convoys. | Rarely used — helicopter delivery is low altitude. |
| **RBK-250** | Cluster Bomb Dispenser | Area | Concentrations of soft targets. | Area denial. Dispensed from low altitude pass. |

### Pods & Systems
| System | Type | Capability | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Radiopeleng (9S469)** | ATGM Guidance System | Radio Command guidance for Shturm/Ataka | Weapons Operator station. Keep crosshair on target from launch to impact. |
| **GOES-342 FLIR Turret** | Electro-Optical / IR | Day/Night targeting | Slaved to Shturm/Ataka guidance. Can operate independently for recon. |
| **SPO-15 Beryoza RWR** | Radar Warning | Radar threat detection | Same as Su-25T. Audio/visual alerts. |
| **UV-26 Chaff/Flare** | Countermeasures | IR/Radar decoys | Dispensed automatically or manually. |
